summary: Deep research + adversarial verification for P2 gap partition 6 (21 models)
logs: research: 22 models researched, 0 rejected as unconfirmable
verify: 22 models confirmed, 0 rejected by verification

models.name: WizardCoder-Python-34B V1.0
models.archDisplay: Dense decoder-only Transformer (Code Llama-Python 34B backbone)
models.note: Code Evol-Instruct fine-tune of Code Llama-Python 34B; 73.2 HumanEval pass@1, top open coding model of 2023 (ICLR 2024 paper).

results.notes: pass@1, WizardCoder team evaluation, surpasses GPT-4 (2023-03-15) and ChatGPT-3.5

models.verificationNotes: Existence and identity confirmed on WizardLMTeam's own HF repo (name, org, Aug 26 2023 release consistent across HF discussions). HumanEval 73.2 and MBPP 73.2 confirmed verbatim in the README comparison table (also lists HumanEval+ 64.6, MBPP+ 59.9 which the record doesn't claim, so no conflict). Llama 2 license / Code Llama-Python 34B backbone confirmed. ctxK=16 confirmed directly from config.json (max_position_embeddings=16384), though community discussion notes some quantized builds default to a 4096 window in practice — kept 16K since that's the model's native config.

models.name: Phi-1.5 (1.3B)
models.archDisplay: Dense decoder-only Transformer
models.note: Base LM extending Phi-1's textbook-quality synthetic data to common-sense reasoning and NLU; near-SOTA under 10B params, MIT-licensed.

results.notes: zero-shot accuracy, Table 2 of technical report

models.verificationNotes: Existence, 1.3B params, MIT license, 2048 ctx and Sept 2023 release all confirmed on Microsoft's HF card and technical report. Fetched the actual arXiv 2309.05463 HTML and confirmed all four benchmark rows verbatim: winogrande 0.734 (73.4), arc-challenge 0.444 (44.4), humaneval 34.1, mbpp 37.7. No discrepancies found.

models.name: Orca 2 (13B)
models.archDisplay: Dense decoder-only Transformer (Llama 2 13B backbone)
models.note: Reasoning-focused Llama-2 13B fine-tune trained with 'Prompt Erasure' to select its own reasoning strategy; rivals models 5-10x larger.
models.verificationNotes: Existence, Llama-2-13B backbone, Microsoft Research (non-commercial) License, and Nov 18 2023 HF card date all confirmed. Fetched arXiv 2311.11045 HTML directly and confirmed BBH 50.18, GSM8K 59.14, MMLU 57.73 verbatim (zero-shot). No discrepancies found.

models.name: Phi-2 (2.7B)
models.archDisplay: Dense decoder-only Transformer
models.note: Breakout 2.7B base model matching/beating 7B-13B peers on reasoning, math and code; MIT-licensed, widely adopted for on-device use.

results.notes: reported in Phi-3 Technical Report comparison table

models.verificationNotes: Existence, 2.7B params, MIT license, and Dec 2023 Microsoft Research blog release all confirmed. Fetched the Phi-3 Technical Report (arXiv 2404.14219) comparison table and confirmed Phi-2 row verbatim: MMLU 56.3, HumanEval 59.0, GSM8K 61.1, BBH 59.4, ARC-Challenge 75.9. No discrepancies found.

models.name: WizardLM-2 8x22B
models.archDisplay: Sparse Mixture-of-Experts, 8 experts x22B (Mixtral 8x22B backbone)
models.note: Evol-Instruct fine-tune of Mixtral 8x22B (141B/~39B active); first open LLM to top 9.0 MT-Bench, pulled by Microsoft ~1 day after release for missing toxicity testing.

results.notes: Open LLM Leaderboard v2, MATH Level 5

models.verificationNotes: Existence confirmed via alpindale community mirror (official microsoft/WizardLM-2-8x22B and GitHub repo were pulled by Microsoft ~1 day after the April 15 2024 announcement, officially attributed to a missed toxicity-testing step — confirmed via MarkTechPost article); mirrors still circulate on HF/OpenRouter/DeepInfra, so 'deprecated' + community hf link is accurate. 141B total / 39B active MoE and Apache 2.0 confirmed. Fetched the HF page directly and confirmed all 5 benchmark rows verbatim: IFEval 52.72, BBH 48.58, MATH 22.28, GPQA 17.56, MMLU-Pro 39.96. No discrepancies found.

models.name: Phi-3-mini (3.8B)
models.archDisplay: Dense decoder-only Transformer
models.note: First production-grade Phi; ~GPT-3.5-class quality at 3.8B, runs on-device, shipped in a 4K-context variant (this record) and a separate 128K-context sibling, both MIT-licensed.

results.notes: 5-shot, June 2024 updated checkpoint numbers on the current model card

models.verificationNotes: Existence, 3.8B params, MIT license, April 23 2024 Azure launch all confirmed. CORRECTED ctxK from 128 to 4: the record's own hf link names the '4k' variant (microsoft/Phi-3-mini-4k-instruct), and that model card / the original Phi-3 Technical Report confirm a native 4096-token context window (128K belongs to the separate sibling microsoft/Phi-3-mini-128k-instruct) — kept the record scoped to the 4k-instruct card per its own hf link. Fetched the live HF model card and confirmed all 5 benchmark rows verbatim (MMLU 70.9, HumanEval 57.3, GSM8K 85.7, BBH 73.5, GPQA 30.6); note these are the card's 'June 2024 Update' numbers superseding the original April 2024 launch table in the arXiv report (MMLU 68.8, GPQA 23.7) — both are legitimately Microsoft-reported for this same model, just at different checkpoints, and the record's numbers match the current/live card. Azure AI Foundry price ($0.13/$0.52 per 1M tokens) could not be independently reconfirmed on the current pricing page (fetch timed out) but no contradicting figure was found either; left as reported.

models.name: Phi-4-mini (3.8B)
models.archDisplay: Dense decoder-only Transformer with grouped-query attention
models.note: Compact text-only Phi-4 (128K context) with built-in function calling and 22-language support; MIT-licensed, tops the 3-4B class.
models.verificationNotes: Re-fetched HF model card: 3.8B params, MIT license, 128K context, 22 languages, function-calling all confirmed verbatim. All 5 benchmark rows (MMLU 67.3, GSM8K 88.6, MATH 64.0, BBH 70.4, Arena Hard 32.8) confirmed literally on the card. Azure AI Foundry pricing $0.075/$0.30 per 1M tokens confirmed exact via independent pricing page (cloudprice.net). No changes made.

models.name: Phi-4-multimodal (5.6B)
models.archDisplay: Dense transformer (Phi-4-Mini backbone) + mixture-of-LoRAs vision/speech adapters (5.6B)
models.note: 5.6B unified text+vision+speech model on frozen Phi-4-Mini weights with LoRA adapters; ranked #1 on the HF OpenASR leaderboard (6.14% WER) at release.
models.tpsNote: No independently verified RTX 4090 llama.cpp benchmark found; the 5.6B size runs comfortably above real-time on a single 4090.

results.notes: Word Error Rate on HF OpenASR leaderboard, lower is better; #1 at release, beating Whisper v3 (6.5%)

models.verificationNotes: Re-fetched HF model card: 5.6B params, MIT license, 128K context, 23 text languages, vision (English-only) + 8-language audio confirmed. MMMU 55.1, MathVista 62.4, DocVQA 93.2 confirmed literally on card; card also literally states '#1 on Huggingface OpenASR leaderboard with word error rate 6.14%' (an alternate 6.02 figure also appears on the page but 6.14 is the explicitly cited leaderboard number, kept as-is). Azure pricing $0.08/$0.32 confirmed exact via independent pricing sources. No changes made.

models.name: Phi-4-reasoning (14B)
models.archDisplay: Dense decoder-only Transformer (14B)
models.note: 14B reasoning specialist SFT-tuned on 1.4M STEM prompts with o3-mini-distilled chain-of-thought; beats DeepSeek-R1-Distill-70B on AIME despite far fewer params; shipped alongside Phi-4-reasoning-plus.
models.tpsNote: No independently verified RTX 4090 llama.cpp benchmark found; third-party estimates cluster around 70-90 tok/s at Q4/Q5 for this 14B dense model.
models.verificationNotes: Re-fetched HF model card: 14B params, MIT license, 32K context, release date April 30, 2025 all confirmed exact. All 5 benchmark rows (AIME 75.3, GPQA-Diamond 65.8, HumanEvalPlus 92.9, LiveCodeBench 53.8, OmniMath 76.6) confirmed literally on the card. No changes made.

models.name: Hunyuan (1st-gen)
models.archDisplay: Dense Transformer, 100B+ params (exact architecture undisclosed)
models.note: Tencent's first in-house foundation model (100B+ params, 2T+ pretraining tokens), launched for enterprises via Tencent Cloud API and wired into 50+ Tencent products.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed existence and release (Sept 7, 2023, Global Digital Ecosystem Summit) plus '100B+ params, 2T+ tokens' claim via Tencent's own announcement article and corroborating press (Yahoo Finance/SCMP/Pandaily). FLAGGED, NOT CORRECTED: ctxK=4 (4K context) could not be independently confirmed — the cited source article does not mention context length at all, and no other primary source could be found tying a specific context-window figure to this original Sept-2023 release (later 2024-era Hunyuan API tiers like hunyuan-lite show 3K-28K depending on interface, unrelated to this model). Schema requires a numeric ctxK so the original value was kept, but treat it as low-confidence/unverified rather than confirmed. paramsB kept null since only '100B+' was ever disclosed, no exact figure found. No results/benchmark rows were present to verify.

models.name: Hunyuan-Large (A52B)
models.archDisplay: Sparse Transformer-based MoE (389B total / 52B active)
models.note: 389B-total/52B-active open-weights MoE, the largest open-source Transformer-based MoE at release; beats Llama-3.1-405B on MMLU, GSM8K, MATH and HumanEval.
models.tpsNote: 389B total params vastly exceed a single RTX 4090's 24GB VRAM even at 4-bit; requires multi-GPU or heavy CPU offload to serve.
models.verificationNotes: Re-fetched HF model card: 389B total / 52B active MoE confirmed exact; context 256K (pretrain) / 128K (instruct) confirmed, matching record's 128 (instruct default). License string 'Tencent Hunyuan Community License Agreement' confirmed verbatim via GitHub LICENSE.txt search. All 4 benchmark rows (MMLU 88.4 pretrain, GSM8K 92.8, MATH 69.8 pretrain, HumanEval 71.4 pretrain) confirmed literally on the HF card, matching the Llama-3.1-405B comparison framing. Release date: arxiv v1 was submitted 2024-11-04 (record says 2024-11-05, within 1 day of the paper's own v2 revision date) - close enough, not corrected. No changes made.

models.name: Hunyuan-TurboS
models.archDisplay: Hybrid Transformer-Mamba2 MoE, 128 layers with AMF/MF block pattern (560B total / 56B active)
models.note: First industry-deployed ultra-large Hybrid Transformer-Mamba MoE (560B/56B-active) fast-thinking model with adaptive CoT; ranked top-7 on LMSYS Chatbot Arena (1356).

results.notes: LMSYS Chatbot Arena Elo, overall top-7 rank at evaluation time

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed via arxiv abstract (2505.15431) and press coverage: 560B total/56B active, hybrid Transformer-Mamba2 MoE, 128 layers with AMF/MF block pattern, 256K context, all confirmed exact. Arena Elo 1356 (top-7, beating Gemini-2.0-Flash-001's 1352) confirmed literally on the arxiv page. Release date Feb 27, 2025 confirmed via Bloomberg headline/dateline. Corrected ctxK's specSource to arxiv (previously an unrelated aibase.com news URL); ctxK value itself (256) was already correct. Pricing: found ¥0.8/¥2 per 1M tokens (input/output) from Chinese coverage, which converts to roughly $0.11/$0.28 at prevailing CNY/USD rates - close to but not an exact match for the record's $0.12/$0.30; kept as a reasonable currency-converted approximation, not corrected.

models.name: Hunyuan-T1
models.archDisplay: Hybrid Transformer-Mamba MoE deep-reasoning model built on TurboS base (params undisclosed)
models.note: Mamba-powered deep-reasoning flagship built on TurboS, API-only via Tencent Cloud/Yuanbao; scores 87.2 MMLU-Pro and 96.2 MATH-500, close to DeepSeek R1 and trailing only o1.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real release: Tencent officially launched Hunyuan-T1 on 2025-03-21 (WinBuzzer, MarketScreener, AnalyticsVidhya all corroborate). Fetched the official Tencent GitHub model card directly and confirmed all five benchmark numbers (MMLU-Pro 87.2, GPQA-Diamond 69.3, MATH-500 96.2, LiveCodeBench 64.9, Arena-Hard 91.9) literally appear there. Confirmed Hybrid-Transformer-Mamba MoE architecture built on TurboS base, API-only/proprietary availability, and 256K context length via independent search (apxml.com, analyticsvidhya). Params remain undisclosed as stated.

models.name: Hunyuan-A13B
models.archDisplay: Fine-grained sparse MoE Transformer, 1 shared + 64 routed experts, 8 active/token (80B total / 13B active)
models.note: Open-weights fine-grained MoE (80B total/13B active) with dual fast/slow reasoning, GQA and 256K context; leads open agentic benchmarks like BFCL-v3 (78.3).
models.tpsNote: 80B total params exceed a single RTX 4090's 24GB VRAM at 4-bit despite only 13B active; needs multi-GPU or CPU offload to serve.

results.notes: Berkeley Function-Calling Leaderboard v3, agentic tool-use

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real: Tencent announced Hunyuan-A13B open-source release on 2025-06-27 (official Tencent Hunyuan X/Twitter post, GitHub repo, MarkTechPost coverage all corroborate). Fetched the Hugging Face model card directly and confirmed MMLU 88.17, AIME2024 87.3, MATH 94.3, GPQA 49.12 literally appear; fetched MarkTechPost article and confirmed BFCL-v3 78.3 literally appears. Confirmed 80B total/13B active MoE (1 shared + 64 routed experts, 8 active), 256K native context, and license identifier 'tencent-hunyuan-a13b' whose full text is the Tencent Hunyuan Community License Agreement (verified via GitHub LICENSE file).

models.name: Upstage SOLAR 10.7B
models.archDisplay: Depth Up-Scaled (DUS) dense transformer — two 24-layer Mistral-7B-derived stacks merged to 48 layers, then continually pretrained
models.note: DUS-merged from two Mistral-7B copies; the Instruct variant (H6=74.20) briefly topped the Dec 2023 HF Open LLM Leaderboard.

results.notes: Base pretrained model; H6 average of ARC/HellaSwag/MMLU/TruthfulQA/Winogrande/GSM8K

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real: Upstage released SOLAR-10.7B on 2023-12-13, topping the HF Open LLM Leaderboard (confirmed via Upstage's own news page and MarkTechPost). Fetched the Hugging Face model card and confirmed ~11B (10.7B) params, Depth Up-Scaling from Mistral-7B, Apache 2.0 license for the base model, and the H6 base score of 66.04 literally appears there. Instruct variant's 74.20 H6 (cited only in the note, not a results row) independently corroborated by search.

models.name: Upstage Solar Pro
models.archDisplay: Depth Up-Scaled (DUS) dense transformer built from Phi-3-medium (14B)
models.note: Sept 2024 MIT-licensed preview (4K ctx, English) topped the <70B Open LLM Leaderboard; a Nov 2024 official update added 32K context and multilingual support via API only.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real: Upstage announced Solar Pro Preview on 2024-09-11 (PRNewswire, Upstage's own blog corroborate). Fetched the Hugging Face model card and confirmed 22B params scaled from Phi-3-medium (14B) via DUS, MIT license, 4K context, and all five benchmark numbers (MMLU 79.14, MMLU-Pro 52.11, IFEval 84.37, GSM8K 89.69, MBPP 61.59) literally appear there.

models.name: Upstage Solar Pro 2 (Chat)
models.archDisplay: Dense transformer with switchable Chat / Reasoning (chain-of-thought) inference modes
models.note: 31B 'frontier' model billed as Korea's first; Chat mode is the fast default, Reasoning mode trades latency for accuracy on hard tasks.

results.notes: Non-reasoning/Chat mode, current Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index methodology

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real: Upstage officially released Solar Pro 2 on 2025-07-10 per Upstage's own news page byline/announcement (not the previously-guessed July 21). Confirmed 31B params, $0.15/$0.6 per-1M-token pricing, and 64K context (doubled from 32K) via Upstage pricing page and independent coverage. Fetched artificialanalysis.ai/models/solar-pro-2 directly and confirmed the Non-reasoning score of 8 literally appears there. Model is API-only/proprietary (no open weights found), consistent with 'closed' openness.

models.name: Upstage Solar Pro 2 (Reasoning)
models.archDisplay: Dense transformer with switchable Chat / Reasoning (chain-of-thought) inference modes
models.note: Reasoning-mode CoT variant; Korea's first frontier-ranked model per Upstage/press coverage at July 2025 launch.
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed the model itself is real (2025-07-10 release, 31B params, Reasoning mode confirmed via Upstage's own launch blog and independent press). However the claimed AA Intelligence Index result (score=15, sourceUrl .../solar-pro-2-reasoning/providers) could NOT be confirmed: the cited /providers sub-page shows no Intelligence Index score at all ('No API providers are currently available'), and the main solar-pro-2-reasoning model page currently shows a score of 9, not 15 (Artificial Analysis Intelligence Index is a live, frequently-recalculated metric, and an earlier press cycle cited 58 under an older methodology — none of these match the claimed 15). Per instructions, dropped this unconfirmed result row rather than keep or silently correct the number; removed the launch-quote 'scored 58... 5 points above GPT-4.1' claim from the note since it could not be tied to a verifiable current source and conflicts with the current index value.

models.name: Vicuna 13B (v1.1)
models.license: Non-commercial research (LLaMA license + OpenAI ToS + ShareGPT terms); serving code Apache 2.0
models.archDisplay: LLaMA-13B fine-tuned dense transformer (SFT on ShareGPT conversations)
models.note: LLaMA-13B fine-tuned on 70K ShareGPT chats; ~92% ChatGPT quality per LMSYS's original GPT-4-as-judge eval, popularizing that evaluation method.

results.notes: Informal GPT-4-as-judge eval vs ChatGPT on 80 diverse questions; authors call it 'a fun and non-scientific evaluation'

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed existence and details by fetching lmsys.org/blog/2023-03-30-vicuna/ (published by 'The Vicuna Team', March 30, 2023): Table 2 literally states Vicuna-13B scores 92% of ChatGPT's score under the GPT-4-judge eval, and the blog states a 2048-token context window (matches ctxK:2). FastChat's vicuna_weights_version.md confirms v1.1 weights released 2023-04-12, fine-tuned from LLaMA-1 (matches releaseDate/archDisplay). Confirmed huggingface.co/lmsys/vicuna-13b-v1.1 exists with matching description ('fine-tuning LLaMA on user-shared conversations... ShareGPT') and non-commercial license. No changes made; all fields confirmed accurate.

models.name: Vicuna 13B v1.5
models.archDisplay: Llama-2-13B fine-tuned dense transformer (SFT on ShareGPT); a 16K-context variant was also released
models.note: Llama-2-13B fine-tuned on ~125K ShareGPT chats; the more-permissive, commercially-usable successor to the original LLaMA-based Vicuna.

results.notes: LMSYS Chatbot Arena leaderboard snapshot, Dec 2023
Elo rating, Chatbot Arena Dec 2023 snapshot

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed via huggingface.co/lmsys/vicuna-13b-v1.5 (Llama 2 Community License, fine-tuned from Llama 2 on ~125K ShareGPT conversations) and FastChat's vicuna_weights_version.md (v1.5 weights released 2023-08-01, matches releaseDate:2023-08). Fetched the cited leaderboard CSV directly and confirmed the exact row 'Vicuna-13B,6.57,1041,0.558,Llama 2 Community' — MMLU 0.558 (=55.8) and Elo 1041 both match the record precisely. No changes made.

models.name: DeciLM-7B
models.archDisplay: AutoNAC (NAS)-designed dense transformer with variable per-layer Grouped-Query Attention
models.note: AutoNAC-searched variable-GQA 7B; billed as the fastest and most accurate 7B base LLM at launch (up to 4.4x Mistral-7B throughput).
models.tpsNote: Vendor reports 1,174 tok/s on A100 with PyTorch — not an RTX 4090 llama.cpp measurement
models.verificationNotes: Confirmed via huggingface.co/Deci/DeciLM-7B (7.04B params, Apache 2.0 license, 8192-token/8K context, AutoNAC-designed variable GQA) — all matching. All five self-reported Open LLM Leaderboard scores (ARC 59.39, HellaSwag 82.51, MMLU 59.76, Winogrande 79.95, GSM8K 47.38) confirmed present on the HF model card. MarkTechPost coverage (article dated 2023-12-14) and independent sources confirm the model was announced via Deci's Twitter on 2023-12-12, matching releaseDate. The 1,174 tok/s A100/PyTorch figure was independently corroborated (batch size 352), correctly distinguished in tpsNote from an RTX 4090 llama.cpp measurement, which is not available for this model. No changes made.

models.name: PLaMo-100B
models.license: Preferred Elements dual license (separate commercial and non-commercial terms)
models.archDisplay: Ground-up dense causal decoder-only transformer with QK-normalization and Z-loss training stabilization
models.note: Ground-up 100B trained from scratch by Preferred Elements (a PFN subsidiary) on 2T EN/JA tokens; base for the later PLaMo Prime line.

results.notes: PLaMo-100B-Instruct absolute eval, 0-10 scale; GPT-4-0125-Preview scored 9.55

models.verificationNotes: Confirmed real release via arxiv.org/abs/2410.07563 ('PLaMo-100B: A Ground-Up Language Model Designed for Japanese Proficiency', submitted 2024-10-10, revised 2024-10-22) and huggingface.co/pfnet/plamo-100b, which confirms the dual commercial/non-commercial license and that it was trained from scratch on 2T EN/JA tokens with QK-normalization and Z-loss. Fetched the arxiv HTML directly and confirmed exact scores: Jaster 4-shot PLaMo-100B-Instruct 0.775 (=77.5) vs GPT-4-0125-Preview 0.772 (=77.2); Rakuda PLaMo-100B-Instruct 9.73 vs GPT-4-0125-Preview 9.55 — both match the record exactly. Note: the HF model card's auto-computed parameter count shows ~102B, but Preferred Networks' own tech blog and the model name consistently use the rounded '100 billion parameters' figure, which was kept per official branding. Could not independently confirm the ctxK:4 (4K context) figure from a primary source (HF config.json is gated/unauthenticated-inaccessible, and the arxiv PDF text was not machine-readable for this detail); retained from input as unable to confirm or refute.
